expectations.text: Classifies all three as Sensitive Personal Information under §1798.140(ae): precise geolocation (within ~1,850 ft radius), health data, and biometric information processed for identification purposes
expectations.evidence: 'generally defined as geolocation data that identifies a consumer's or device's location within a radius of 1,850 feet (approximately 1/3 mile) or less. This is one of the enumerated SPI categories under Cal. Civ. Code § 1798.140.' Correctly classifies health data and biometric-for-identification as SPI as well, with the correct precision radius matching the assertion.

expectations.text: Explains the Right to Limit (§1798.121): consumers may limit SPI use to what is necessary to perform the services — requiring a 'Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information' link unless the business only uses SPI within the permitted purposes
expectations.evidence: 'Consumers have the right to direct you to limit your use of SPI to only what is necessary to perform the services reasonably expected... you must provide a clear "Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information" link... and honor consumer requests.'

expectations.text: Correctly notes the authentication carve-out analysis: SPI used solely for purposes necessary to provide the requested service (e.g., biometric authentication the user enrolled in) may fall within permitted purposes, changing whether the Limit link is required
expectations.evidence: 'If your use of the SPI is strictly limited to providing the core service the consumer requested (e.g.,... using facial biometric data only to authenticate the account holder) and you don't use it for secondary purposes... you may not need to offer the limit-use link.'

expectations.text: Requires notice-at-collection disclosures: SPI categories, purposes, retention periods per category, and whether sold/shared
expectations.evidence: Section 3 lists required disclosures: categories of SPI collected, purposes, whether used beyond necessary/sold-shared, retention criteria for each category.

expectations.text: Flags heightened risk posture: data minimization/purpose limitation duties (§1798.100(c)), and risk-assessment obligations for high-risk processing under the CPPA regulations where applicable
expectations.evidence: 'collection, use, retention, and sharing of SPI must be reasonably necessary and proportionate to achieve the purposes for which it was collected' (purpose limitation/minimization) and 'you likely fall within the scope of CPRA's risk assessment and potentially annual cybersecurity audit requirements... which apply to businesses whose processing presents significant risk.'
